rationale: The only direct linkage is a single DOI-matched reference entry, with no in-text citation context or quoted surrounding text to show how the 2005 paper is used. Although both notes concern conjoint/preference elicitation and predictive validity (the citing paper benchmarks preference-measurement methods; the cited paper shows incentive-aligned conjoint effects), the current evidence is too sparse to confidently classify this as either clearly substantive or clearly incidental.
